item.title: Sensex Slumps Nearly 2% as All Sectors Decline; Metal Sector Leads Losses
item.description: The Indian equity market witnessed a broad-based sell-off on 13 Mar 2026, with the Sensex plunging 1.89% to close at 74,600.74 amid widespread sectoral weakness and subdued investor sentiment. Market breadth was severely negative as all 38 sectors declined, led by a sharp fall in metals, while foreign institutional investors remained net sellers. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Defensive and Cyclical Divergence
item.description: The large-cap segment, represented by the BSE 100 index, has experienced a notable downturn, declining by 2.07% on the day and registering a sharper 3.36% fall over the past five trading sessions. This performance reflects a broad-based weakness with a stark advance-decline ratio of just 0.03x, as 97 stocks declined against only 3 advancing, underscoring the prevailing bearish sentiment among heavyweight constituents. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Broad Market Weakness
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, experienced a notable decline on 13 Mar 2026, reflecting broader market pressures. The index fell by 2.54% on the day and has declined 2.29% over the past five trading sessions, signalling a challenging environment for mid-sized companies amid sectoral headwinds and weak market breadth. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Faces Downward Pressure Amid Broad Market Weakness
item.description: The small-cap segment, represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has experienced a notable decline, falling by 2.47% on the day and registering a 1.12% drop over the past five trading sessions. Despite this recent weakness, select stocks within the segment have bucked the trend, highlighting a mixed performance landscape amid broad market pressures. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Sensex Drops Over 1500 Points as Market Sentiment Turns Bearish Across Sectors
item.description: Indian equity markets witnessed a sharp sell-off on 13 Mar 2026, with the Sensex plunging 1,529.10 points or 2.01% to close at 74,505.32. The decline was broad-based, with all 38 sectors on the BSE ending in the red and market breadth severely skewed towards declines. Mid and small caps also suffered heavy losses, signalling widespread risk aversion among investors amid weak global cues and subdued domestic sentiment. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Faces Broad-Based Decline Amid Weak Market Breadth
item.description: The small-cap segment, as represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has experienced notable weakness recently, declining by 2.85% on the day and 1.5% over the past five trading sessions. This downturn reflects broader market pressures and sector-specific challenges, with a significant majority of stocks in the segment retreating amid subdued investor sentiment. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Broad Market Weakness
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, has experienced a notable downturn, declining by 2.81% on the day and registering a 2.55% drop over the past five trading sessions. Despite this broad weakness, select stocks within the segment have bucked the trend, highlighting a mixed performance landscape driven by sectoral disparities and uneven market breadth. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Defensive and Cyclical Divergence
item.description: The large-cap segment, represented by the BSE 100 index, has experienced a notable downturn, declining 2.25% on the day and 3.54% over the past five trading sessions. This broad-based weakness is underscored by a stark advance-decline ratio, with only 4 stocks advancing against 96 declining, signalling widespread selling pressure. Within this challenging environment, Tata Consumer Products emerged as the best performer with a modest gain of 1.28%, while Larsen & Toubro (L&T) lagged significantly, falling 7.01% and marking the segment’s worst performance. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Sensex Drops 1.6% as Market Sentiment Turns Bearish; Metals Sector Leads Declines
item.description: The Indian equity markets witnessed a sharp sell-off on 13 Mar 2026, with the Sensex plunging 1.60% to close at 74,820.65, extending losses throughout the session. Market breadth was overwhelmingly negative as all 37 sectors declined, led by a steep fall in the metals segment. Large caps traded largely flat with select exceptions, while mid and small caps bore the brunt of the downturn amid subdued global cues and cautious investor sentiment. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Faces Broad Decline Amid Defensive and Cyclical Stock Divergence
item.description: The large-cap segment witnessed a pronounced downturn on 13 Mar 2026, with the BSE 100 index declining by 1.84% on the day and a sharper 3.13% drop over the past five sessions. This broad-based weakness was underscored by a lopsided advance-decline ratio, where only 6 stocks advanced against 94 decliners, signalling pervasive selling pressure. Notably, Tata Consumer emerged as the best performer with a modest 2.26% gain, while Larsen & Toubro (L&T) lagged significantly, falling 5.68% despite a recent upgrade in its technical rating. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Faces Broad-Based Decline Amid Weak Market Breadth
item.description: The small-cap segment, represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has experienced a notable decline, falling by 2.55% on the day and registering a 1.2% drop over the past five trading sessions. This downturn reflects a broader market correction, with sectoral performances and breadth indicators signalling a cautious outlook for investors in this space. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Broad Market Weakness
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, has experienced notable weakness over recent sessions, declining by 2.46% on the day and 2.21% over the past five days. Despite this broad downturn, select stocks within the segment have bucked the trend, highlighting a mixed performance landscape amid challenging market conditions. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Eicher Motors Ltd Hits Intraday Low Amid Price Pressure on 13 Mar 2026
item.description: Eicher Motors Ltd experienced a notable decline today, touching an intraday low of Rs 6,770.7, reflecting a 2.93% drop as the stock faced sustained selling pressure amid broader market weakness and sectoral headwinds. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Jindal Steel Ltd. Hits Intraday Low Amid Price Pressure on 13 Mar 2026
item.description: Jindal Steel Ltd. experienced a notable decline today, touching an intraday low of Rs 1,158.3, reflecting a 5.46% drop as the stock faced significant price pressure amid broader market weakness and sectoral downturns. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Avenue Supermarts Sees Significant Open Interest Surge Amid Mixed Market Signals
item.description: Avenue Supermarts Ltd (DMART) has witnessed a notable 13.47% increase in open interest in its derivatives segment, signalling heightened market activity and shifting investor positioning. This surge accompanies a modest price gain and rising volumes, reflecting a complex interplay of bullish and cautious sentiment among traders. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Ortel Communications Ltd Surges to Upper Circuit Amid Strong Buying Pressure
item.description: Ortel Communications Ltd (BZ series) witnessed a remarkable surge on 13 Mar 2026, hitting its upper circuit price limit of ₹1.54, marking a maximum daily gain of 4.76%. This sharp rally was driven by robust buying pressure amid subdued sector performance and a notable decline in investor participation, signalling a potential shift in market sentiment for the micro-cap media and entertainment stock. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Blue Star Ltd. Sees Significant Open Interest Surge Amid Market Volatility
item.description: Blue Star Ltd., a mid-cap player in the Electronics & Appliances sector, has witnessed a notable surge in open interest (OI) in its derivatives segment, signalling heightened market activity and shifting investor positioning. Despite a recent price decline, the stock’s derivatives market reveals increased participation, suggesting evolving directional bets amid sectoral and broader market pressures. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: ABB India Ltd Sees Sharp Open Interest Surge Amid Strong Price Momentum
item.description: ABB India Ltd has witnessed a significant surge in open interest in its derivatives segment, reflecting heightened market activity and shifting investor sentiment. The stock’s recent performance, coupled with increased volume and rising investor participation, suggests a strong directional bias among traders, positioning ABB as a key focus in the Heavy Electrical Equipment sector. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Ola Electric Mobility Ltd Sees Exceptional Volume Amid Continued Downtrend
item.description: Ola Electric Mobility Ltd (symbol: OLAELEC) emerged as one of the most actively traded stocks on 13 Mar 2026, registering a total traded volume exceeding 1.52 crore shares. Despite this surge in activity, the stock continued its downward trajectory, closing near its 52-week low and underperforming key moving averages, signalling persistent selling pressure and investor caution in the small-cap automobile sector. Time: 3 hours ago
